generatedBy: /audit skill v2 · Session 75 infrastructure rubric override + app release/security/game-depth lenses
summary: Premise-verified Session 75 audit: replace client-authored match truth with attested result evidence, make release and persistence posture fail closed, close transport and process-safety blind spots, restore reachable product depth, and remove false-green workflow and parsing surfaces without fabricating external evidence.

sample.method: three in-process read-only audit lanes plus direct main-agent live verification
sample.scanned: result/progression, stores, Worker routes, SSE and AI paths, client reachability, scripts, workflows, deploy chain, public surfaces, and release gates
sample.baseline: doctor blockingFailing 0; GitHub main CI green; focused release tests green; sitemap 10/10; launch NO-GO

evidenceBoundary: Local tests cannot satisfy real human alpha, revenue, DNS, email-delivery, Obelisk RP, staging parity, deployed-header, Core Web Vitals, or visual theme evidence.
Registry and local project status agree on public-unlaunched/game, but registry cost metadata remains internally contradictory.
No sibling repo tree will be edited; registry/protocol fixes travel as Ark cargo.
AGPL upstream obligations and VaultSpark-original rights posture remain unchanged.

items.title: Make one canonical result certificate the only input to progression, telemetry, and archives
items.why: GameServer accepts client-supplied winner and allPlayersStats at a non-strict 50% IP quorum, then advances durable systems from the first matching payload.
items.recipe: Canonicalize and hash the complete result; require a strict active-IP majority; validate winner membership and a bounded complete roster; emit an immutable config/turn/result certificate; route archive and progression through it; test splits, tamper, duplicate IPs, incomplete rosters, and idempotency.

L1.recipe: Require strict majority and validate the roster.

L2.recipe: Ship the certificate and route all local result consumers through it.

L3.recipe: Replay the turn log server-side and sign a derived final-state certificate.

executionLog.note: Strict unique-IP quorum, immutable SHA-256 result certificate, certificate-gated archive/progression, and tamper/idempotency tests.

items.title: Close the private-game creation authorization and allocation bypass
items.why: Any non-empty admin header authorizes private creation without a bearer actor, and GameManager replaces or allocates IDs without collision or worker-cap protection.
items.recipe: Require verified actor or exact admin token for every create path; reject existing IDs; add per-actor/IP and worker capacity; return stable 401/403/409/429/503 outcomes; test adversarial paths.

L1.recipe: Require exact authorization and reject collisions.

L2.recipe: Add quotas, capacity, outcomes, and tests.

L3.recipe: Persist signed allocation receipts across workers.

executionLog.note: Exact actor/admin authorization, collision and capacity guards, bounded admission, stable status codes, and adversarial tests.

items.title: Model every canonical release gate and make footer and health evidence executable
items.why: Readiness can report ready without staging, parity, Brevo, Obelisk, live web/theme proof, or approval; missing alphaGate passes; leaf footers are incomplete; no footer manifest exists; protocol health path is absent.
items.recipe: Add a provenance/freshness gate contract that fails closed; require alphaGate ready; expose /_health; complete leaf footers; add a canonical manifest and wire its probe into doctor, release evidence, and CI; keep external gates red until observed.

L1.recipe: Fail closed and expose /_health.

L2.recipe: Add provenance plus footer probe and release wiring.

L3.recipe: Ingest signed staging, email, identity, browser, and approval receipts.

executionLog.note: Eight-gate provenance/freshness contract, dual live /_health routes, executable footer manifest, doctor integration, and honestly blocked external evidence.

items.title: Turn hidden process-local persistence into a state-scope ledger and fail-closed database posture
items.why: PostgreSQL logs fallback while pool remains non-null after failure, readiness ignores persistence, and advertised persistent features use process-local maps.
items.recipe: Create awaited disabled/connecting/ready/failed database state; block configured failure; declare durability, replication, retention, recovery, and probe ownership for each store; compile the State Scope Ledger into readiness and runtime integrity.

L1.recipe: Fix pool state and surface posture.

L2.recipe: Compile the ledger into runtime and release checks.

L3.recipe: Migrate all meta stores to one transactional durable repository.

executionLog.note: Awaited database posture and fail-closed configured persistence plus a scope/durability/recovery ledger compiled into readiness and runtime evidence.

items.title: Turn Worker route sprawl into a typed testable route manifest
items.why: Worker is roughly 4,000 lines with dozens of inline schemas and routes, while authorization tests scan strings and can pass without semantic enforcement.
items.recipe: Extract high-risk create, readiness, experiment, and AI route groups behind shared actor/schema middleware; register typed method/path/auth/mutation/rate/evidence policy; derive semantic contract tests and client typing.

L1.recipe: Manifest routes and replace marker tests.

L2.recipe: Extract high-risk groups and derive semantic tests.

L3.recipe: Generate OpenAPI/client contracts and split every router.

executionLog.note: Executable exact-path policy manifest for create/readiness/runtime/three experiment mutations/all AI routes, shared semantic authorization, startup drift assertions, typed client auth, and contract tests.

items.title: Make the stale shell deployment chain dry-runnable immutable and cleanup-bounded
items.why: The active deployment wrapper calls an unproven March-era SSH chain with no successful Deploy history, while the updater prunes every unused image without retention budget.
items.recipe: Consolidate one entrypoint for deploy and promote; add dry-run tests, immutable digest validation, bounded image retention, explicit health/staging inputs, and shell syntax checks; do not claim deploy evidence.

L1.recipe: Add dry-run, digest validation, and bounded cleanup.

L2.recipe: Consolidate entrypoints and add contract tests.

L3.recipe: Exercise signed canary to staging to production promotion.

executionLog.note: Consolidated immutable staging/promote entrypoints, digest validation, health checks, dry run, rollback, bounded retention, shell syntax and doctor contracts without fabricated deployment evidence.

items.title: Close every Windows spawn-policy blind spot and remove the broken v5 renderer branch
items.why: Doctor reports green while a dynamic raw child_process import lacks windowsHide, its v5 target is missing, and TypeScript test and perf spawners sit outside the scanner.
items.recipe: Route all spawns through safe-spawn with process.execPath and argv; remove the dead v5 branch; scan dynamic imports plus owned TS/test/perf roots; add fixtures for every former bypass; retain doctor enforcement.

L1.recipe: Remove raw and dead paths and fix perf.

L2.recipe: Expand the scanner and regression suite.

L3.recipe: Move execution behind a typed command manifest.

executionLog.note: Owned JS/TS/test/perf dynamic-spawn scanning, safe-spawn routing, dead v5 removal, bypass fixtures, and doctor enforcement.

items.title: Stop client identity and chat leakage and bound outbound notification delivery
items.why: ChatModal logs sender and recipient objects plus message keys, while Discord embeds accept user content, permit mentions, lack timeout, and expose no posture.
items.recipe: Remove content-bearing logs; normalize and truncate webhook fields; disable mention parsing; enforce abort timeout; record bounded privacy-safe posture; test injection, timeout, and no-content logging.

L1.recipe: Remove sensitive logs and disable mentions.

L2.recipe: Add bounded delivery, sanitization, posture, and tests.

L3.recipe: Emit signed privacy-safe notification receipts.

executionLog.note: Content-bearing log removal, bounded/sanitized webhook payloads, mention suppression, timeout/concurrency/drop posture, and privacy tests.

items.title: Remove Dependabot red noise and disable the dormant upstream release path
items.why: The last ten PR-description runs failed because trusted automation is forced through human prose and milestone gates, while a dormant workflow can target hard-coded OpenFront infrastructure.
items.recipe: Give trusted automation a machine-verifiable PR contract while preserving substantive CI; use attributable names; disable or replace legacy fan-out with immutable VaultFront promotion; add workflow contract tests.

L1.recipe: Exempt trusted automation and disable legacy triggers.

L2.recipe: Add machine PR validation and promotion tests.

L3.recipe: Auto-batch compatible dependency trains behind staging.

executionLog.note: Machine-verifiable automation attribution, preserved CI, removed legacy fan-out, immutable promotion contract, and workflow tests.

items.title: Replace 100 ms full VaultFront projection broadcasts with invalidation-driven deltas
items.why: Every tick rebuilds sites, convoys, five route previews, structure scans, and a full payload even when objective state is unchanged, with no dedicated benchmark.
items.recipe: Separate static and dynamic projections; invalidate on objective, convoy, and structure changes; emit digest-deduplicated snapshots at bounded UI cadence with immediate critical events; add payload/tick p95 and equivalence tests.

L1.recipe: Dedupe unchanged payloads and throttle.

L2.recipe: Add invalidation, caching, cadence controls, and perf tests.

L3.recipe: Ship typed binary deltas with reconciliation.

executionLog.note: Bounded cadence, immediate critical invalidation, collision-safe digest plus canonical equality, p95/build/publish/dedupe posture, and equivalence tests.

items.title: Extend bounded transport policy from WebSockets to both Server-Sent Event buses
items.why: StreamingBus and NarratorBus accept unbounded subscribers and ignore response backpressure, retaining slow consumers outside the claimed transport posture.
items.recipe: Add per-game/per-worker/IP ceilings, bounded buffered events, drain timeouts, slow-consumer eviction, counters, and deterministic tests; include SSE policy and live counts in the runtime passport.

L1.recipe: Cap clients and evict failed writes.

L2.recipe: Add queues, timeouts, counters, tests, and passport evidence.

L3.recipe: Move fan-out to a shared durable event stream.

executionLog.note: Per-game, per-worker, and per-client admission; bounded buffers; drain timeouts; slow-consumer eviction; counters; runtime-passport evidence; deterministic tests.

items.title: Bind AI outputs and caches to authenticated canonical match evidence
items.why: Oracle caches player-specific predictions by player-count bucket, while recap and coach accept unverified narratives that can poison confident game-key output.
items.recipe: Key caches by canonical input digest; authenticate evidence routes; derive winner, events, and stats from result certificates; validate provider output with Zod; add abort deadlines, bounded retention, and poison/malformed-output tests.

executionLog.note: Certified archived inputs, requester binding, canonical digest cache keys, bounded TTL/LRU caches, provider deadlines, strict Zod output validation, and poison/tamper tests.

items.why: Achievements, season pass, clans, tournaments, experiment dashboard, and achievement toasts exist but are not imported, mounted, navigable, or exercised end to end.

skipped.reason: Verified high-value architecture, but a shared PostgreSQL migration/backfill/retention rollout is external-state work; this arc will make process scope explicit and keep launch unready rather than ship a partial ledger.
All declared runtime origins are NXDOMAIN and there is no successful runtime deploy evidence. Local staging is the registered gate; no production/SPARKED flip is authorized.
Credential is READY, but no live staging origin exists to register and the generated React stub is incompatible with the Lit app. Keep quarantine and ship RP cargo only after origin exists.
Credential is READY, but the project domain is NXDOMAIN; delivery cannot be verified without DNS. Public copy stays explicitly unverified.
No automation, synthetic event, or agent action can substitute for real human behavior or a real checkout/supporter event.
Coverage ratchet exists and passes, but enabling all:true across a 3,760-line Worker before extraction would create an unusable gate. The selected security extraction/tests are the root-first step.
Base-image/cloudflared digest and signature pinning requires a separate trust-gated artifact selection pass; container parity is selected now, mutable artifact pinning is retained as explicit debt.
Tracked binary and missing Go CI are verified debt but rank below exploitable mutation/WebSocket/release-truth defects in this infrastructure pass.
L2 removes first-payload and 50% quorum defects; replay needs a validated engine, not a false-authority claim.
The scope ledger makes promises explicit; provisioning, backfill, and failover need live PostgreSQL evidence.
Staging, human sessions, Brevo, Obelisk, live web/theme evidence, revenue, and founder approval cannot be fabricated.
The control-plane fix must travel by signed Ark cargo, never a sibling-tree edit.
Major upgrades require per-package trust and compatibility trains; selected fixes require no install.
Typed manifest and high-risk extraction are safer than a one-shot composition-root rewrite.
L2 removes first-payload and 50% quorum defects; replay needs a validated engine, not a false-authority claim.
